description:
The WaterRower A1 is an entry-level, hand-crafted rowing machine with an ash wood and aluminum frame, featuring a single-rail design. It utilizes a WaterFlywheel for natural, low-impact resistance, designed to simulate on-water rowing while being foldable for vertical, space-saving storage. It includes an A1 monitor to track basic metrics. 
Key Features and Specs
Frame & Build: Hybrid design with solid ash wood and an aluminum monorail for the seat, designed for, but not limited to, commercial, group exercise environments.
Performance: Uses a WaterFlywheel, which provides a smooth,,, quiet,,, and natural,,,,, rowing,,, stroke,,,.
Monitor: A1 Quick-Start LCD monitor displaying, distance, intensity (m/s, mph, /500m, /2km), watts, calories, stroke rate, and duration.
Storage & Portability: Can be stored vertically (roughly 3 units take up the same floor space as one bike) and features built-in wheels for easy transport.
Price (UK): Generally priced around £849–£899. 
Differences from Other WaterRowers
Monorail: The A1 has a single-rail seat, whereas other models (like the S4) have dual rails.
Monitor: Uses the more basic A1 monitor rather than the advanced S4 or SmartRow

Details and features
The Reebok i-Run 4.0 is a compact, home-friendly treadmill designed for space-saving and ease of use. It features a quick-release folding mechanism, transport wheels, and a 2 hp motor reaching speeds up to 16 km/h. With 12 pre-set workouts, 3 goal modes, and a 4° incline, it supports varied and challenging training. An adjustable mount for tablets/phones allows multimedia engagement and access to fitness apps and online communities, enhancing the workout experience.

1.25 HP continuous motor size

Feedback: Speed, distance, time, calories, pulse, bodyfat, steps
